local_names.malay: Teh Hijau Madu Limau Bunga Liar M

aliases: Teh Hijau Madu Limau Bunga Liar M

malay: Teh Hijau Madu Limau Bunga Liar M

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical medium-sized serving of M-Wildflower Honey Lime Green Tea is approximately 500 grams, equivalent to one large cup.

nutrient_source.title: Tea, green, brewed, sweetened
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient data for a generic sweetened green tea used as an estimate for this specific branded beverage. Values adapted from USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 170940.

gi_source.title: University of Sydney GI Database — analogous estimate based on sweetened green tea and honey-sweetened beverages
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this branded product. GI estimated at 49 (low) based on analogous sweetened green tea beverages and honey-sweetened drinks documented in the University of Sydney GI Database. Green tea itself has a negligible GI; honey has a GI of approximately 55–58 (medium-low); lime juice contributes negligible glycemic load. The blended beverage, diluted with brewed tea and water, is estimated at GI 49 (low category, threshold <55). Glycemic load per 500 g serving calculated as (49 × 8.8 × 5) / 100 = 21.6 g.

hypertension.guidance: This beverage has a very low sodium content of approximately 1 mg per 100 g, making it a suitable choice for individuals managing hypertension when consumed without additional salt or high-sodium accompaniments.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar and carbohydrate content with added honey and lime syrup
type2_diabetes.guidance: With approximately 8.8 g of sugar per 100 g and a 500 g serving delivering around 44 g of sugar, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit or avoid this beverage, as the high free-sugar load can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High added sugar content contributing to excess caloric intake
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While low in fat and sodium, the high added sugar content of this beverage can contribute to elevated triglycerides and excess caloric intake, both of which are risk factors for cardiovascular disease; moderation and portion control are advised.

llm_training.natural_language_description: M-Wildflower Honey Lime Green Tea is a popular Taiwanese-style sweetened cold beverage sold at KOI Thé, a well-known bubble tea chain with outlets in Singapore. The drink is prepared by brewing green tea and blending it with wildflower honey and fresh lime juice, resulting in a refreshing, floral, and lightly citrusy flavour profile. The 'M' designation refers to the medium cup size. The beverage is served chilled and is enjoyed as a refreshing snack or dessert drink. Nutritionally, it is a source of simple carbohydrates and sugars derived primarily from honey and any added syrups, with negligible protein and fat. Its glycemic index is estimated at 49 (low category), though the high total sugar load per serving makes it a concern for individuals managing blood glucose or caloric intake. It is naturally dairy-free and gluten-free. The green tea base provides trace antioxidants including catechins, though the clinical significance in this diluted, sweetened form is limited.

confidence_reason: Nutrient data is estimated from a generic sweetened green tea entry in USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 170940) due to the absence of specific branded product data in approved primary sources. Glycemic index is an analogous estimate based on honey-sweetened and green-tea-based beverages from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this product was found.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Populated all mandatory fields from RESULT template; added nutrient data from USDA FoodData Central; added multilingual names and aliases; added ontology codes; added health context and LLM description.
Iteration 2: Corrected JSON formatting; ensured alias consistency between flat list and aliases_by_language; added zero-macro justifications in notes; added sentinel value documentation.

Iteration 3: Corrected glycemic_index from 55 to 49 to align with low GI category (GI < 55); corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' to match the revised GI value; updated gi_source notes to document the revised analogous estimate basis; recalculated glycemic_load_per_serving to 21.6 g; updated notes field to document the GI correction.
